podcast.name: The Curious Bartender Podcast
podcast.description: Long-form conversations with the leading minds in drinks, spanning history, science, culture, and craft.
podcast.host_bio: Tristan Stephenson is a bar and drinks entrepreneur, bestselling author of The Curious Bartender book series, and podcast host. He co-founded Fluid Movement in 2009, launching award-winning venues including Purl, Worship Street Whistling Shop, and Black Rock.

episodes.title: Hannah & Siobhan - London Cocktail Week, The Pinnacle Guide, London, Community
episodes.description: Hannah Sharman-Cox and Siobhan Payne discuss their journey building London Cocktail Week over 16 years and creating the Pinnacle Guide, a Michelin-inspired rating system that awards one, two, or three pins to bars. Topics include relocating the event from October to March, the evolution of London's cocktail scene, and a new venture called Curate Your City.

episodes.title: Agostino Perrone - Connaught Bar, Creativity, Italian Hospitality, Career, Photography
episodes.description: Agostino Perrone, Director of Mixology at London's acclaimed Connaught Bar, explores building lasting legacies in hospitality. The conversation covers Italian cultural influences on service, the famous Martini Trolley, balancing creativity with consistency, team leadership, and photography as personal meditation.

episodes.title: Tom Oliver - Cider & Perry Making, Orchards, Varieties, Fermentation, Cider Appreciation, Herefordshire, Agriculture
episodes.description: Tom Oliver, a respected cider and perry maker, discusses the challenges of crafting quality perry, orchard management, wild fermentation, keeving processes, and regional distinctions. The episode includes tastings and explores cider as a wine alternative.

episodes.title: Julian & Matilda Temperley - Somerset Cider Brandy, Craft Distilling Since 1987, PGI, Apples, Agriculture
episodes.description: The Temperley family, operating the UK's first independent distillery in living memory, share their four-decade journey reviving English apple spirits. Topics include licensing history, orchard management, distillation techniques, cask aging, and future development.

episodes.title: Henry Jeffreys - Drinking the British Empire, Churchill, Fortified Wine, Bordeaux, Rum, Gin, Cider, Legendary Drinkers
episodes.description: Drinks historian Henry Jeffreys explores how the British Empire shaped the way the world drinks, examining fortified wines, rum's naval history, gin culture, and misconceptions about Churchill's consumption habits.

episodes.title: Tom Dyer - Flair Bartending: History, Culture, Training, Competition, Legends of Flair
episodes.description: Tom Dyer, founder of the World Flair Association and flair competition champion, chronicles flair's nearly 400-year history from TGI Fridays through modern competition, discussing training, bar magic, and contemporary innovators.

episodes.title: Sunny Hodge - Wine Cynicism, Debunking Terroir, Soil Science, Rootstocks, Wine Education, Diogenes The Dog, Cynicism
episodes.description: Wine author and bar owner Sunny Hodge takes a scientific approach to viticulture, examining soil composition, root biology, rootstocks, and challenging romantic wine narratives through technical explanations.

episodes.title: Peter Dorelli - Savoy Hotel, Being a Fugitive, Lessons from 65 Years, Harry Craddock, Retiring
episodes.description: Peter Dorelli, sixth head bartender at the American Bar at the Savoy, shares 65 years of hospitality experiences, including immigration challenges, legendary colleagues, and lessons from the industry's golden era.

episodes.title: Claire Warner - Non-Alc, Social Connection, Healthspan, Happiness, Dry January
episodes.description: Explores non-alcoholic beverages, social connection, health considerations, and the cultural phenomenon of reduced alcohol consumption.

episodes.title: Will Hawkes - 500 Best Pubs, Pub History, Cask Ale, Craft Beer, Guinness
episodes.description: Beer and pub writer Will Hawkes discusses British pub culture's current state, pub history, cask ales, and the industry's evolution in the 2020s.

episodes.title: Remy Savage - Are Drinks Art? Bar Concepts, Philosophy of Creativity, AI in Hospitality, Designing Drinks, Motivation, Baguettes
episodes.description: Bar conceptualist Remy Savage explores high-concept bar design, the philosophical intersection of drinks and art, creativity philosophy, and hospitality's future with emerging technologies.

episodes.title: Best of 2025 - 12 Conversations that Shaped My Thinking
episodes.description: A curated compilation highlighting twelve standout conversations from the year that significantly influenced the host's perspective.

episodes.title: Gabe Cook - How Cider is Made, Appreciating Cider, Cider History, Growing Apples, Cider Producers, English Cider, American Cider
episodes.description: Cider expert Gabe Cook provides deep insights into cider production, appreciation methodologies, historical context, and regional variations across English and American cidermakers.

episodes.title: Simon Difford - Diffordsguide.com, CLASS Mag, 28 Years of Publishing, Influential Bars & Bartenders, London, Sauce Guides, Jiggers, Daiquiris
episodes.description: Publishing entrepreneur Simon Difford discusses nearly three decades creating Diffordsguide.com, CLASS Magazine, cocktail literature, influential London venues, and bartending culture evolution.

episodes.title: Dr. Johnny Drain - Hacking Flavour, Microbes, Food Systems, Gut Health, Chocolate without Cocoa, Future of Food
episodes.description: Materials scientist-turned-fermentation expert Dr. Johnny Drain explores flavor innovation through microbiology, food system transformation, and developing alternative protein-based products.
